page.content: In the context of Stripe Inc's performance in the Fintech and Digital Payments sector, the analysis covers the period of 5-Year CAGR in EUR. For the year 2021, revenue was €150M with customer growth at 20%. In 2022, revenue increased to €180M while customer growth declined to 15%. The year 2023 saw further revenue growth to €210M and a reduction in customer growth to 10%. In 2024, revenue is projected to reach €250M with an impressive customer growth of 25%.

lines.text: In the context of Stripe Inc's performance in the Fintech and Digital Payments sector, the analysis covers the period of 5-Year
CAGR in EUR. For the year 2021, revenue was €150M with customer growth at 20%. In 2022, revenue increased to €180M
while customer growth declined to 15%. The year 2023 saw further revenue growth to €210M and a reduction in customer
growth to 10%. In 2024, revenue is projected to reach €250M with an impressive customer growth of 25%.

page.content: The following year, 2025, anticipates revenue of €300M and a customer growth rate of 20%. The overall 5-Year CAGR stands at 26.2%, contributing to a market share of 25%.

lines.text: The following year, 2025, anticipates revenue of €300M and a customer growth rate of 20%. The overall 5-Year CAGR stands

page.content: Additionally, key metrics reveal a 5-Year CAGR for premium growth at 25% with a policy count totaling €300M. Furthermore, a customer growth rate of 20% is noted alongside a retention rate of 85% and a net promoter score of 75. The claims ratio is reported at 60%, while the operating margin is 15%. The customer acquisition cost totals €300M, encapsulating all expenditures. The total for the aforementioned metrics highlights the economic viability and competitive positioning of Stripe Inc within the European market.
